data.title: Amazon adds news tab to Prime Video in user expansion push
data.description:
Amazon.com said on Wednesday it is rolling out a dedicated news tab on its Prime Video streaming service, which will become available to all U.S. customers for free by the end of the year.

The section, hosting news networks that include ABC News Live, CBS News 24/7, LiveNOW from Fox, CNN Headlines and NBC News NOW, will cover a broad selection of topics such as world, national and regional news.

The e-commerce giant is looking to make it more convenient for customers to access live networks and grow its user base as it grapples with strong competition from rivals looking to increase market share.

Separately on Wednesday, Amazon also announced a subscription channel where customers can watch a selection of Sony Pictures movies and shows.

Sony Pictures Core is available on Prime Video to customers in the U.S. and Canada for $6.99 per month.

data.title: Aegon NV Cut to Neutral From Buy by UBS
Bond investors warned of Hassett as potential Fed chair over rate-cut fears, FT reports
data.description:
Bond investors have expressed concerns to the U.S. Treasury that Kevin Hassett could aggressively cut interest rates to align with President Donald Trump's preferences, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday, citing several people familiar with the conversations.

The discussions with executives at major Wall Street banks, asset management giants and other big players in the U.S. debt market took place in November, before Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent held his second round of interviews with candidates to replace Jerome Powell, the report said.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report. The White House and Treasury did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comments.

The bankers and investors were worried that Hassett could agitate for indiscriminate rate cuts even if inflation continues to run above the Fed's 2% target, the report added.

Members of the Treasury Borrowing Advisory Committee were among those consulted, FT said, adding that when Hassett met the group earlier this year, he focused on White House priorities, including Mexican drug cartels, rather than the market.

"The president will continue to nominate the most qualified individuals to the federal government, and until an announcement is made by him, any discussion about potential nominations is pointless speculation," a White House spokesperson told the newspaper.

Trump on Tuesday said he will announce his pick to succeed Powell as Fed chair early next year, extending a months-long selection process despite previously claiming he had already decided on the candidate.

Trump said that a potential Federal Reserve chair was present as he introduced White House economic advisor Kevin Hassett at a White House meeting.

data.title: Apple's design executive Alan Dye to join Meta
data.description:
Meta Platforms has hired Alan Dye, Apple's longtime head of user interface design, a spokesperson for Apple confirmed on Wednesday.

Dye, who will join Meta as chief design officer on December 31, started at Apple in 2006 and has led its human interface design team since 2015.

During his tenure, he helped shape the look and feel of Apple's flagship products, including the Vision Pro headset, iPhone X and Apple Watch, and oversaw major redesigns of its operating systems and apps.

Apple said veteran designer Stephen Lemay will succeed Dye. "Steve Lemay has played a key role in the design of every major Apple interface since 1999," CEO Tim Cook said in a statement.

data.title: Key facts: Charles Schwab stock soars 150%; seeks mergers; wins award
data.description:
• Charles Schwab (SCHW) saw a 150% stock value rise after introducing commission-free trading, reflecting industry trends amid growing brokerage accounts at rivals like Robinhood.1
  • Charles Schwab CEO Rick Wurster revealed at the NEXT conference that the firm is seeking mergers and acquisitions to improve client services and capabilities.2
  • Charles Schwab was honored at the 2025 TrustFinance Performance Awards for its outstanding stock and investment brokerage, highlighting its strong performance and commitment to responsible practices.3

data.title: Key facts: Cardinal Health prioritizes high ROI; officers surrender shares
data.description:
• Cardinal Health's capital deployment strategy focuses on high return on investment (ROI) opportunities, prioritizing shareholder returns and selective mergers and acquisitions.1
  • On December 2, 2025, Cardinal Health (CAH) officers reported surrendering 1,949 shares to cover option exercise costs and tax liabilities, as filed with the SEC.2

data.title: Key facts: Box Inc. expands Amazon partnership; Q3 profit declines
data.description:
• Box Inc. has expanded its partnership with Amazon through a multi-year deal and announced new integrations with Anthropic, Mistral AI, and Google Cloud Marketplace.1
  • Box Inc. posted a Q3 profit of $12.1 million, or 5 cents per share, down from $12.9 million last year. Adjusted earnings were 31 cents, missing expectations of 32 cents.2

data.title: Key facts: Boeing shares rise 10.15%; debt concerns and FTC scrutiny remain
data.description:
• Boeing (BA) shares rose 10.15% to $205.38 as regulatory issues near resolution and delivery outlooks improve, with a trading volume of 22,570,318 shares.12
  • Boeing has significant debt, with $8 billion in payments due next year and $3 billion from acquiring Spirit AeroSystems, complicating its recovery under new CEO Kelly Ortberg.3
  • The FTC is scrutinizing Boeing's acquisition, citing worries about higher costs and less competition for Airbus in commercial and military aircraft markets. The EU approved the deal in October.4

data.title: Key facts: BlackRock's IBIT ETF hits $1B in first-day trading; Sells BTC, ETH
data.description:
• BlackRock's new crypto ETF, IBIT, saw $1 billion in trading volume on its first day at Vanguard, indicating strong market interest in cryptocurrency.1
  • BlackRock has sold more Bitcoin and Ethereum despite a crypto market rebound, with Ethereum up over 7% to $3,100, raising questions about their selling motives.2
  • BlackRock is the second-largest Bitcoin holder, possessing 628,868 BTC, trailing only Satoshi Nakamoto and surpassing other notable holders such as Strategy.3

data.title: Key facts: Bank of America to Offer Bitcoin Advice; Predicts S&P 500 Gains
data.description:
• Bank of America will allow 15,000 advisers to suggest Bitcoin allocations of 1-4% to clients starting January 2026, marking its entry into cryptocurrency investments.123
  • Bank of America will cover four spot bitcoin ETFs, including BlackRock's IBIT, starting early next year, improving cryptocurrency investment access for clients.4
  • Bank of America predicts modest S&P 500 gains for 2026, targeting 7,100, lower than the Wall Street median of 7,500, due to stressed consumers affecting stock performance.5

data.title: Key facts: BILL Holdings Chairman and VP increase shareholdings
data.description:
• On December 2, 2025, BILL Holdings Chairman Rene A. Lacerte bought 15,396 shares, raising his total to 2,749,856, including 82,826 directly and 2,667,030 indirectly.1
  • Mary Kay Bowman, VP of BILL Holdings Inc, bought 11,282 shares on December 2, 2025, raising her total to 41,928 shares, per an SEC filing.2

data.title: Key facts: Annox Capital to sell 40 restricted securities; Booking.com boosts S&P 500
data.description:
• Annox Capital LLC, a Booking Holdings (BKNG) shareholder, filed a Form 144 on December 2, 2025, to sell 40 restricted securities under a prearranged trading plan.1
  • Booking.com played a role in the positive momentum of the S&P 500, contributing to the market's uplift alongside other technology stocks such as Intel and Qualcomm.2

data.title: Key facts: Amgen reports strong growth; BMO raises price target to $372
data.description:
• Amgen reported strong revenue and volume growth driven by six key product areas, including Repatha, TEZSPIRE, and its rare disease portfolio, which showed significant momentum.1
  • BMO Capital has increased its price target for Amgen (AMGN) to $372.00 per share, up from the previous target of $335.00.2

data.title: Key facts: American Eagle Outfitters raises Q4 forecast; price targets up
data.description:
• American Eagle Outfitters raised its Q4 operating income forecast to $155-$160 million, with expected comparable sales growth of 8%-9%. Full-year adjusted operating income is now $303-$308 million.1
  • Several firms raised price targets for American Eagle Outfitters (AEO): Citigroup to $23, B of A to $18, UBS to $31, Telsey to $25, Barclays to $20, and JP Morgan to $20.2
  • American Eagle Outfitters is boosting visibility with celebrity campaigns, collaborating with Sydney Sweeney and Travis Kelce's Tru Kolors to attract younger shoppers in a competitive market.3

data.title: Key facts: Amazon Q3 revenue rises 13.4% to $180.2B; new AI chip boosts AWS
data.description:
• Amazon's Q3 revenue hit $180.2 billion, up 13.4% year-on-year, beating forecasts. EPS also exceeded estimates, boosting stock by 5.1% to $234.50.1
  • Amazon's new Trainium 3 AI chip delivers over four times the performance of its predecessor, cutting AI training costs by 50% and energy use by 40%, enhancing AWS capabilities.234
  • Amazon Web Services (AWS) introduced AWS AI Factories, offering AI infrastructure for businesses and governments. AWS CEO announced 3.8 gigawatts of new data-center capacity, outpacing rivals.56

data.title: Key facts: AT&T ends diversity programs; executives buy shares; files lawsuit
data.description:
• AT&T is ending its diversity programs to gain FCC approval for a $1.02 billion acquisition of wireless spectrum licenses from U.S. Cellular, reflecting changing legal requirements.12
  • On December 2, 2025, AT&T executives disclosed share acquisitions. CEO John T. Stankey bought 7,570 shares, totaling 1,172,940. COO Jeffery McElfresh acquired 3,494 shares, totaling 736,360.3
  • AT&T has filed a lawsuit against generic drug makers, including Teva and Sandoz, claiming they conspired to raise drug prices since 2009, causing significant overcharges for its health plan.4

data.title: Key facts: AI customers drive 12% of Datadog's revenue; CEO files Form 144
data.description:
• AI native customers represent 12% of Datadog's revenue, highlighting strong retention and the effectiveness of its flexible, scalable platform.1
  • On December 2, 2025, Datadog Inc CEO Pomel Olivier filed a Form 144 with the Securities and Exchange Commission to propose the sale of 15,337 restricted shares of the company.2
